title: What Is Retatrutide (GLP-3)? The Triple Agonist Obesity Drug Explained
h2_questions: What is retatrutide?
Why is retatrutide called GLP-3?
Is there a GLP-3 pill?
What makes retatrutide a triple agonist?
Who is developing retatrutide?
How does the GGG tri-agonist mechanism work?
key_data_points: Retatrutide is an investigational once-weekly injectable drug developed by Eli Lilly
It is a triple agonist: activates GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously
Informally called 'GLP-3' in consumer media; this is not an official pharmacological classification
No oral (pill) form of retatrutide is currently in development or approved
Retatrutide is not the same molecule as GLP-1; the 'GLP-3' nickname is colloquial
INN (International Nonproprietary Name) is retatrutide; Eli Lilly has not announced a brand name

TRIUMPH-1 is the Phase 3 cardiovascular outcomes and weight loss trial
Triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon agonism (GGG) distinguishes retatrutide from dual agonists
Phase 2 responder rates: >80% of participants lost ≥10% body weight at highest dose
Phase 3 TRIUMPH program spans multiple trials including cardiometabolic endpoints
